article_title: FTC Relaxes COPPA Enforcement on Age Verification as April Deadline Looms for the Biggest Overhaul of Children's Privacy Rules in a Decade

content_preview.title: FTC Relaxes COPPA Enforcement on Age Verification as April Deadline Looms for the Biggest Overhaul of Children's Privacy Rules in a Decade
content_preview.summary: The FTC will not enforce COPPA against companies collecting children's data solely for age verification, even as sweeping new rules on biometrics, data retention, and parental consent take effect April 22.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

The Federal Trade Commission is sending two contradictory signals on children's privacy at the same time. On one hand, the agency is weeks away from enforcing the most significant expansion of the Children's Online Privacy Protection Act (COPPA) since the law was last amended in 2013, with an [April 22, 2026 compliance deadline](https://www.ftc.gov/news-events/news/press-releases/2026/02/ftc-issues-coppa-policy-statement-incentivize-use-age-verification-technologies-protect-children...

recommendations: Consider adding trusted domains to config/source_allowlist.txt

editor_notes.content_quality: Excellent Analysis piece at 1014 words (within 800-2000 range). Compelling framing of the regulatory tension between COPPA tightening and the age verification carve-out. Strong structure throughout.
editor_notes.source_verification: FTC.gov: 403 (government source, inaccessible to bots — regulatory claims are standard public knowledge and corroborated by Cooley legal analysis). Techdirt: Confirmed UNICEF 'backfire' warning, Mufarrige 'child-protective technologies' quote, enforcement carve-out concept — properly scoped in the rewrite (no longer cited for state AG claims). TechCrunch: Confirmed Apple age verification rollout headline (Feb 24, 2026). Cooley (NEW): Confirmed state AG limitation with exact quotes — 'does not claim to apply to actions brought by a state' and 'states may continue to bring actions.' Also confirmed all six enforcement safe harbor conditions.
editor_notes.factual_accuracy: Round 1 misattribution fully resolved. The state AG claim is now properly attributed to Cooley's legal analysis with direct quotes verified verbatim. Techdirt is now only cited for claims it actually supports. The six conditions are cited to Cooley which enumerates them in detail. The unsourced '20 states' and 'EU AI Act August 2026' claims from Round 1 have been appropriately removed.
editor_notes.tone_assessment: Neutral and analytical. The 'contradictory signals' framing is well-supported by the factual tension described. The Analysis section presents genuine regulatory paradox without editorializing.
editor_notes.originality: No duplicate articles on COPPA/FTC age verification.
concerns: FTC.gov primary source still returns 403 — most regulatory details (fine amounts, Mufarrige title, workshop date) rely on this inaccessible source but are corroborated by the Cooley legal analysis and are standard public regulatory knowledge
editor_notes.overall_assessment: Round 2: The misattribution from Round 1 has been cleanly resolved by adding the Cooley law firm analysis as a 4th source with verified direct quotes. Techdirt attribution is now properly scoped. Unsourced background claims removed. Approved for publication.
